CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF UPTIME RESOURCES, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 2.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WARREN BRANSON'S AND DOUGLAS MOUNT'S MOTION TO STAY ALL PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. THE LABOR COMMISSIONER HAS PRIMARY JURISDICTION TO HEAR DEFENDANTS' WAGE CLAIMS. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO STAY THE INSTANT ACTION PENDING THE BERMAN HEARING. (FARMERS INS. EXCHANGE V. SUP.CT. (1992) 2 CAL.4TH 377, 390.) THE LABOR COMMISSIONER HAS AUTHORITY TO HEAR DEFENDANTS' EMPLOYEE COMPLAINTS AND TO DETERMINE WHETHER DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO THEIR WAGE CLAIMS BASED ON THEIR EMPLOYMENT STATUS. (LAB. CODE 98.) THE LABOR COMMISSIONER HAS THE ADMINISTRATIVE EXPERTISE TO DETERMINE DEFENDANTS' WAGE CLAIMS. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DELAY CAUSED BY THE BERMAN HEARING DOES NOT RENDER IT INADEQUATE. (FARMERS INS. EXCHANGE V. SUP.CT., SUPRA, 2 CAL.4TH AT 390, FN. 9.) PLAINTIFF FILED THE INSTANT ACTION ON JUNE 12, 2013, AND NO TRIAL DATE HAS BEEN SET. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2014 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-22-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jul-08-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-19-2013 Continued To May-07-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay All Proceedings
Off Calendar Dec-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS WARREN BRANSON'S and DOUGLAS MOUNT'S Motion To Stay All Proceedings IS Granted. The Labor Commissioner has primary jurisdiction to hear Defendants' wage claims. The Court exercises its discretion to stay the instant action pending the Berman hearing. (Farmers Ins. Exchange v. Sup.Ct. (1992) 2 Cal.4th 377, 390.) The Labor Commissioner has authority to hear Defendants' employee complaints and to determine whether Defendants are entitled to their wage claims based on their employment status. (Lab. Code 98.) The Labor Commissioner has the administrative expertise to determine Defendants' wage claims. The Court finds that the delay caused by the Berman hearing does not render it inadequate. (Farmers Ins. Exchange v. Sup.Ct., supra, 2 Cal.4th at 390, fn. 9.) Plaintiff filed the instant action on June 12, 2013, and no trial date has been set. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay All Proceedings
Off Calendar Nov-21-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants WARREN BRANSON'S and DOUGLAS MOUNT'S Motion To Stay All Proceedings IS Continued to December 11, 2013 to give Defendants the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Defendants must deliver courtesy copies of the reply papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than November 21, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and opposition papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2013 Continued To Jan-08-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
